Title: A near-gapless genome assembly of Pseudocydonia sinensis uncovers unique phenylpropanoid pathways

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes rhamnosylation of flavonol-3-O-glucosides to form rutin-type flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: upregulated with fruit development and activated by PsMYBODO1, contributing to increased rutin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsRT3 was identified as a key structural gene in the rutin biosynthetic pathway of P. sinensis. 2. Its expression increased toward the S4 fruit stage, matching maximal rutin accumulation. 3. PsMYBODO1 directly bound the PsRT3 promoter in Y1H assays and transient PsMYBODO1 overexpression upregulated PsRT3 expression together with increased rutin content.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PsRT3 native promoter; inducible by PsMYBODO1 in transient overexpression context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in fruit, especially S4 stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in fruit peel during late development (S4)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; RT-qPCR; transient overexpression of PsMYBODO1; yeast one-hybrid assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (S1-S4); transient overexpression incubation at 25°C under 16 h/8 h light/dark
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate terminal-step gene for increasing rutin content in Chinese quince through pathway engineering or regulatory activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as RT3 in the network and functional assays; direct biochemical activity was inferred from pathway annotation rather than enzyme assay.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry step supplying both flavonoid and lignin branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher expression is associated with enhanced flux to both rutin and lignin accumulation during fruit maturation and after PsMYBODO1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsPAL/PAL2 was a major structural gene correlated with PsMYBODO1 expression and phenylpropanoid metabolite accumulation. 2. Expression increased during fruit development, especially by S4. 3. PsMYBODO1 directly bound the PsPAL promoter and transient PsMYBODO1 overexpression elevated PsPAL transcript abundance together with rutin and lignin contents.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PsPAL native promoter; inducible by PsMYBODO1 in transient overexpression context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in fruits; elevated in S4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in fruit peel during late development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; RT-qPCR; transient overexpression of PsMYBODO1; yeast one-hybrid assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (S1-S4); transient overexpression conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong upstream engineering target to elevate total phenylpropanoid flux, though broad effects on multiple branches should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL2 was highlighted in the PsMYBODO1 network, while PsPAL was used in Y1H/overexpression validation; likely refers to one of the PAL family members assayed in detail.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes to their corresponding monolignol alcohols for lignin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: upregulation is associated with increased lignin accumulation during fruit development and after PsMYBODO1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCAD/CAD8 was identified as a key lignin-related structural gene in the phenylpropanoid network. 2. Its expression correlated with PsMYBODO1 and rose during fruit development. 3. PsMYBODO1 directly bound the PsCAD promoter, and PsMYBODO1 overexpression increased PsCAD expression together with lignin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PsCAD native promoter; inducible by PsMYBODO1 in transient overexpression context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in fruits; elevated in S4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in fruit peel during late development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; RT-qPCR; transient overexpression of PsMYBODO1; yeast one-hybrid assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (S1-S4); transient overexpression conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for modulating lignin-associated medicinal/structural traits in fruit peel
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CAD8 was reported in the network; PsCAD was directly tested in Y1H and overexpression assays.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: caffeic acid/caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase or caffeic acid 3-O-methyltransferase (COMT)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates lignin pathway intermediates to promote formation of methoxylated monolignol precursors
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: caffeic acid or related lignin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: ferulic acid or other methoxylated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher expression is associated with increased lignin biosynthesis and is activated by PsMYBODO1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCOMT/COMT1 was a central lignin-related structural gene in the PsMYBODO1 network. 2. Its expression increased toward the S4 stage and after PsMYBODO1 transient overexpression. 3. PsMYBODO1 directly bound the PsCOMT promoter in Y1H assays, with strong regulatory effect inferred from 3-AT tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PsCOMT native promoter; inducible by PsMYBODO1 in transient overexpression context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in fruits; elevated in S4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in fruit peel during late development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; RT-qPCR; transient overexpression of PsMYBODO1; yeast one-hybrid assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (S1-S4); transient overexpression conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target for lignin composition/amount engineering in Chinese quince fruit tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: COMT1 was reported in the network; PsCOMT was directly tested in Y1H and overexpression assays.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: upregulated during late fruit development and after PsMYBODO1 overexpression, supporting increased phenylpropanoid flux to rutin and lignin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsC4H1 was among the major structural genes upregulated in association with phenylpropanoid accumulation. 2. Its transcript abundance increased after PsMYBODO1 transient overexpression. 3. It was connected to PsMYBODO1 in the inferred regulatory network, although direct promoter binding was not functionally confirmed in Y1H within this study.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in fruit, especially late developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in fruit peel during S4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics; co-expression network analysis; RT-qPCR after transient overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (S1-S4); transient overexpression conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: upstream pathway target for boosting general phenylpropanoid output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C4H1 appeared in the PsMYBODO1 co-expression network but direct Y1H evidence was not shown.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: rutin has antibacterial, anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties; lignin contributes to phenylpropanoid-associated fruit traits
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates PsRT3, PsPAL, PsCAD and PsCOMT expression; also associated with upregulation of PsC4H1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phenylpropanoid metabolic flux; flavonoid biosynthesis; lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: identified as a master regulator/key determinant coordinating multiple branches of phenylpropanoid metabolism and enhancing both rutin and lignin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transient overexpression increased rutin and lignin contents in P. sinensis fruits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsMYBODO1 (PsinChr01HBG004760.1) was the most prominent MYB in the yellow WGCNA module correlated with phenylpropanoid metabolites and highly expressed at S4. 2. It is highly expressed in fruits relative to flowers and leaves and localizes to the nucleus and cell membrane. 3. Transient overexpression increased rutin and lignin contents and upregulated PsRT3, PsPAL, PsCAD, PsC4H1 and PsCOMT. 4. Y1H assays confirmed direct binding to promoters of PsRT3, PsPAL, PsCAD and PsCOMT. 5. The study concludes PsMYBODO1 is a master activator of flavonoid and lignin biosynthesis in P. sinensis fruits.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest in fruits, especially late developmental stage S4
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit-enriched; strongly elevated in S4 fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA and co-expression network analysis; phylogenetic analysis; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization assay; transient overexpression in fruit peel; metabolite quantification; yeast one-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely acts within a broader TF network including bZIP and bHLH members; direct complex formation not experimentally demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (S1-S4); transient overexpression for 72 h at 25°C under 16 h/8 h light/dark
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for transient overexpression construct 35S:MYBODO1
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: genome assembly; RNA-seq; metabolomics; co-expression network; NCBI PRJNA1225478
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: possible effects on fruit aroma and texture via broad phenylpropanoid/lignin regulation were suggested but not tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value regulator for molecular breeding and engineering to enhance medicinal phenolics, rutin, lignin-associated traits and potentially aroma; suitable candidate for overexpression, marker-assisted selection, VIGS or CRISPR-based functional optimization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper specifically highlights PsMYBODO1 as the central experimentally validated regulator. Although many other TFs were predicted in the network, they were not functionally characterized and are not core genes here.
